channel.title: Life Log on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Life Log on yexca'Blog

item.title: Playing Honkai: Star Rail Again: Thoughts on the Game Assembly Line
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-3.5-flash

I recently checked my email and found Sparkle (a character in Honkai: Star Rail) quite interesting, so I hopped back into the game. (Yes, Sparkle. I actually forgot to publish this post after writing it, and as usual, I ended up uninstalling the game after playing for just a few days).

Maybe because it’s been a while, but this playthrough felt different. I didn’t feel like a player; instead, I felt like I was trying to reverse-engineer and understand its system architecture.

item.title: Curating My Music, Preserving My Vibes
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

My blog started getting super dry and dull after I began posting more specialized learning content. Seriously, opening it just screamed ‘study time,’ which built up this mental block against writing. I’ve wanted to post other stuff for ages but never got around to it. Well, thanks to some recent events, I’m gonna give a non-tech post a shot.

「まだ雨はやまない」

On 2025-03-23, I grabbed my first physical album: Shigure Ui’s ‘まだ雨はやまない’. The album art, like a photo album, is just too cute!

item.title: My Personal Tea Journey
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

This article was created and last modified between 2022.10.31 and 2024.09.15. It logs the teas I’ve tasted and my personal take on them during this period.

Honestly, towards the later part, I wasn’t really into this hobby anymore, so the logging got pretty casual. But hey, it was a hobby I pursued, and since the article number hit 233, why not drop something that might be a bit interesting? (Thought I’d published this way earlier, kept pushing it back)

item.title: RSS
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview

Introduction

I first encountered RSS back when I was browsing HACG. By the time I actually understood it, RSS was already on the decline. Projects like RSSHub were keeping the flame alive, but I eventually gave up on it due to frequent connection issues. However, as I’ve discovered more blogs, I’ve returned to RSS to stay updated and keep track of what I’ve read.

item.title: Logging My First Grey Hack Mission (Password Acquisition)
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

Intro

Logging my first mission from yesterday. It’s a retelling, so some details might not be exact.

Mission Brief

Passed a test from some shadowy figure, got HackShop access, and then the first mission dropped.

The mission brief: “The client wants the login credentials of the user Daronane. The remote ip of the victim is 54.44.37.52. It’s important that you access the correct machine behind the public ip. The victim’s ip LAN is 172.16.6.7”

item.title: VRChat Auto-Playing Piano in Chinese World
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

I also wrote a model modification tutorial, you can check it out here: https://vrchat.yexca.net/

Intro

Recently, a friend told me that pianos in VRChat can play themselves. So I looked into it and wrote this article to document the process. Note: It seems auto-play only works in PC mode. Of course, you can try casting your desktop in VR (doge).

Without Using a MIDI File

I. Setup

To enable automatic piano playback, you’ll need two software tools and one browser plugin.
